goal: Sort mainline log by activity time and add status filtering

summary.title: Sort log output and filter by status
summary.what: Updated mainline log to sort entries by activity time descending, expose activity_at in JSON output, and support --status filtering while preserving the default limit. Added .codex/ to gitignore for local Codex project state.
summary.why: Log output was unstable because view intents came from map iteration, and users needed a way to focus on proposed, drafting, or merged intents when the default limit hides older entries.
summary.user_goal: Default log ordering should be activity-time descending, keep the default limit at 20, add --status filtering, and use conventional commit messages going forward.

decisions.chose: Sort by parsed RFC3339 activity time descending with intent id as a stable fallback
decisions.rationale: This makes log output deterministic and keeps recently active drafts, proposed work, and merged items visible by recency rather than map iteration.

decisions.chose: Use merged/reverted commit dates when available, otherwise sealed_at or draft last_modified_at
decisions.rationale: The most relevant timestamp depends on the current lifecycle state.

decisions.chose: Add a single --status filter supporting all intent statuses and all/empty as no filter
decisions.rationale: This covers the immediate UX need without changing the default command shape.

rejected.alternative: Group all merged intents before proposed and draft intents
rejected.reason: That can hide recent proposed or draft work behind historical merged entries.

risks: JSON consumers will see a new optional activity_at field on log entries.
followups: Consider supporting comma-separated or repeated --status filters if users need multi-status views.

architectural_claims: mainline log ordering should be deterministic and based on lifecycle activity time, not map iteration order.
behavioral_changes: mainline log defaults to activity-time descending order.
mainline log keeps the configured default limit of 20.
mainline log --status filters entries by a single lifecycle status.
mainline log JSON includes activity_at when available.
.codex/ is ignored as local Codex state.
